rationale: In the Respondent Selection section, the citation is tied directly to concrete design decisions, not just background context. The authors explicitly invoke Homburg et al. (2012) for claims about informant reliability and validity, and for procedures to reduce measurement error in their conjoint-based expert data. This materially informs how a core data input of the paper is constructed and justified.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Homburg et al. (2012) as methodological guidance for expert-response data collection, treating respondents as key informants whose accuracy depends on design choices. It uses that work to justify selecting experienced higher-level informants, aggregating across multiple respondents, and carefully structuring wording and training.
